answer:
\boxed{C}

The phrase **"the birds and beasts . . . enjoyed their existence according to their nature"** aligns with Daoist principles by emphasizing harmony with nature (*wu wei*) and the natural order (*ziran*), central to Daoist thought. This reflects the Daoist belief in spontaneity and non-interference, contrasting with Confucian moral frameworks in the text. The other options focus more on human virtue or divine retribution, which are less core to Daoist philosophy.
